implReport.notes: compile.ts: workspace_id structurally $1/values[0] for EVERY query incl empty/null AST (never from AST; AST field 'workspace_id' rejected by whitelist). FIELD whitelist: attributes.* (p.attributes->>$key), features.counters.* ((pf.counters->>$key)::numeric), 4 scalar feature cols. jsonb KEY names BOUND as params (closes field-name injection). Ops =,!=,>,>=,<,<=,in(=ANY),not in(!=ALL),exists(IS NOT NULL); in/not-in bind whole array as ONE param. Unknown field/op throw. Source discipline: evaluator only writes/deletes source='evaluator' (delete carries AND source='evaluator'); manual.ts owns source='manual'; resolveAudience returns both. Processor: planProcessing keeps profile-upsert-first/feature-second, appends reeval marker; deps runs evaluateRealtimeSegmentsForProfile on SAME tx AFTER feature upsert (post-update features), resolves profile id from (workspace_id,external_id) in tx -> membership+change_log atomic with event. Determinism: unique workspace UUID + event_id namespace per integration file. Scope limited to §8; no broadcasts/automations/dispatch.
implReport.testSummary: Two cold full-suite runs (cache cleared, DATABASE_URL set), both green: 28/28 turbo tasks, 0 test tasks cached, integration ran on real PG :5433. segments 61 tests (compiler: exact §8 AST SQL, workspace_id always $1, jsonb-key+value injection bound only to values[], unknown field/op throw, in/not-in single array param, deep nested and/or/not; diff enter/exit-once; builders; realtime+manual integration enter/exit-once, cross-workspace isolation, manual untouched, both kinds resolve as audiences). batch-eval 11 (core + per-workspace sweep + integration enter/exit-once, idempotent re-sweep, cross-ws isolation). processor segment-reeval-plan 3. No regression: processor 50/1 skipped, db/tenancy/ingest/api/authorizer green.

invariants: Tenant isolation is the top property: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id.
Service-role Lambda code bypasses RLS — it MUST scope by workspace_id in code; RLS is only the backstop for user-context (admin app) reads.
workspace_id is NEVER taken from a client payload — derive it from the API key (ingest) or the authorizer-injected claim (admin API).
Per-profile ordering via MessageGroupId=profile_id; code must be idempotent and order-convergent (progress-before-profile_created still converges).
Idempotency: events.event_id is the dedupe key (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).
Segment rule-AST -> SQL is compiled with a field/operator whitelist, parameterized SQL only, and ALWAYS prepends workspace_id = $ws. Never interpolate raw input.
Local-only: verify against LocalStack + Supabase CLI/Testcontainers. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier. Mock SES (never send real mail).
Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited spec sections require — no extra features, no gold-plating.

criteria: Given an AST, the compiler emits the exact parameterized SQL, ALWAYS injects workspace_id, and REJECTS unknown fields/operators (security-critical).
Crossing a predicate -> exactly one entered; crossing back -> one exited; never matches another workspace's profiles.
Manual segments change only via user edit/CSV and are not touched by the evaluator; both dynamic and manual are usable as audiences.
scope: packages/segments: rule-AST -> parameterized SQL WHERE compiler over profiles JOIN profile_features, with a field/operator WHITELIST and workspace_id = $ws ALWAYS prepended. Reject unknown fields/operators.
Dynamic realtime eval in the processor on profile change; dynamic batch eval (EventBridge-style scheduled entrypoint); membership diff + segment_change_log (entered/exited).
Manual segments: membership rows (source=manual) added/removed by user (hand-pick + CSV import); evaluator never touches them.
phase.specSections: §8 (rule AST + SQL compiler, dynamic realtime/batch, manual), §6 (segments, segment_memberships, segment_change_log)
phase.title: Segmentation engine

implUnits: processor deps.ts wire evaluator into runPlanInWorkspaceTx
plan.notes: Realtime hook: planProcessing extension point; append re-eval AFTER buildFeatureUpsert so it reads updated features (§7 step4->5), in SAME workspace-scoped tx (atomic). Realtime path scopes matching to CHANGED profile (AND p.id=$profile), diffs that one profile's match vs its membership rows (not whole-segment rematerialize). Compiler follows scopedQuery precedent: workspace_id structurally always $1, never from AST (invariant #6, highest-value unit). jsonb KEY names bound as params never concatenated (closes field-name injection). Reuse SqlStatement {text,values}. Integration reuse testutil (adminPool, hasDatabaseUrl, skipIf), evaluator service-role bypass -> in-code isolation. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local event_id namespace (global event_id PK). Whitelist: attributes.* , features.counters.* (::numeric), scalar feature columns. TDD order: compile unit -> diff -> statements -> evaluate+integration -> manual+integration -> processor hook -> batch.

assertions: §8 AST -> exact {text,values}
workspace_id ALWAYS first predicate $1, present even for empty AST
every value is $n (no literal in text); in/not in -> ANY/ALL with array as one param
whitelisted field mapping attributes->>key (key as param), counters->>key::numeric, scalar columns
unknown field THROWS
unknown operator THROWS
injection in field name rejected (whitelist)
injection in value safely parameterized (only in values[])
exists -> IS NOT NULL no extra param
deep nested and/or/not exact text+params balanced parens
comparison ops table-driven
malformed node THROWS no partial SQL
diffMembership: new->entered once, gone->exited once
in both -> neither (idempotent)
empty matched all exited; empty current all entered
duplicates deduped
buildEntered: membership source=evaluator + change_log entered, workspace_id $1
buildExited: delete scoped + change_log exited
membership writes constrained source='evaluator' (never touch manual)
selectActive segments filter workspace+status+kind, EXCLUDE manual
every statement binds workspace_id $1
below predicate -> no match no rows
cross predicate -> exactly one membership(source=evaluator)+one change_log entered
re-eval same state -> no new rows (idempotent)
drop below -> membership removed + one exited
cross-workspace: wsB match never affected by wsA eval; workspace_id=$1 binds wsA
admin pool in-code scoping; unique UUIDs+event_id namespace
in + nested not match real rows end-to-end
enumerate only dynamic_batch active, per-workspace
reuses compileWhere+diff+builders
manual excluded
time-based predicate one entered crossing + one exited back
wsA sweep never touches wsB
re-run unchanged idempotent
profile upsert first, event+feature second (no regression)
segment re-eval appended AFTER feature upsert (reads post-update features)
workspace-scoped, dynamic_realtime only
invoked with changed profile id; no change -> no writes
evaluator skips kind=manual: manual membership unchanged, no change_log
addManualMembers/removeManualMembers workspace-scoped; CSV batch insert
manual member also matching dynamic -> only dynamic(source=evaluator) rows affected
resolveAudience returns members for BOTH manual and dynamic

instructions: Independently RUN the tests (pnpm test for the phase scope), pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint. Record real pass/fail.

Verify EACH acceptance criterion is actually covered by a passing test: ["Given an AST, the compiler emits the exact parameterized SQL, ALWAYS injects workspace_id, and REJECTS unknown fields/operators (security-critical).","Crossing a predicate -> exactly one entered; crossing back -> one exited; never matches another workspace's profiles.","Manual segments change only via user edit/CSV and are not touched by the evaluator; both dynamic and manual are usable as audiences."].

Tenant-isolation review (highest priority): confirm workspace_id scoping in code on every query, that RLS policies are correct, that service-role paths still scope in code, and that workspace_id is never read from client input. For phase5, verify the SQL compiler whitelists fields/operators, parameterizes, and ALWAYS injects workspace_id.
Scope check: flag any feature implemented beyond the cited spec sections.
If integration tests mock Postgres, that is a FAIL (must use real local Postgres per §16A).
Score 0-100. Set testsPass and criteriaAllMet honestly. Give concrete prioritized recommendations.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { score: number, testsPass: boolean, criteriaAllMet: boolean, criteriaCoverage: [{criterion, met:boolean, evidence}], isolationOk: boolean, scopeCreep: string[], recommendations: string[], criticalIssues: string[] }
prompt.role: adversarial principal reviewer + QA engineer verifying spec-fidelity and tenant isolation
prompt.task: Independently verify "Segmentation engine" against its §18 criteria. Re-run the tests yourself; do not trust the self-report.
